title: Dactylorchis cordigera
intro: Dactylorchis cordigera, also known as Dactylorhiza cordigera, is a type of orchid that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammatory diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorchis cordigera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Dactylorchis cordigera is often combined with other herbs to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and promote lung health.
taxonomy: Dactylorchis cordigera is a species of orchid that was previously classified within the genus Dactylorchis. However, the classification of orchids has been revised several times, and Dactylorchis cordigera is now often considered to be a synonym of Dactylorhiza cordigera. Dactylorhiza cordigera is a member of the section Dactylorhiza, which is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as frog orchids. The species is native to Europe and is characterized by its distinctive flowers and growth habits. The classification of Dactylorhiza cordigera may vary slightly depending on the source or taxonomic system used.
